mnemonics.title: FILE ATTRIBUTES
mnemonics.content: FILENAME: NAME, TYPE, LOCATION, SIZE, PROTECTION, TIME (Created/Modified/Accessed)

mnemonics.title: FILE OPERATIONS
mnemonics.content: COWRDS: Create, Open, Write, Read, Delete, Seek

acronyms.expansion: File Control Block - data structure storing file metadata
acronyms.usage: Used by OS to manage file attributes and state

acronyms.expansion: File Allocation Table - file system using table of cluster links
acronyms.usage: Organizes files by storing cluster chains in a table

acronyms.expansion: Index Node - contains file metadata and data block pointers
acronyms.usage: Primary metadata structure in Unix/Linux file systems

acronyms.expansion: System call to prepare file for I/O operations
acronyms.usage: Creates entry in open file table, returns file descriptor

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a cursor blinking at position 0 in a text editor. When you type, characters appear at cursor and cursor moves right. In seek(), you click anywhere in the document and cursor jumps there.
Sequential: cassette tape - must play through songs to reach song 5. Random: CD/MP3 player - press button and jump directly to any track.
Think of file descriptors as seat numbers in a theater. Each process has numbered seats (0,1,2...), and each seat points to a specific file (the actual movie showing).
Think of a buffer as a delivery truck collecting multiple packages before making one trip to the warehouse, instead of making individual trips for each package.

analogies.analogy: A file is like a physical folder in a filing cabinet. Each folder has a label (filename) and contains papers (data). You can open, read, add to, or discard the folder.
Like a library card catalog number. When you request a book, you get a number that the librarian uses to locate and manage the book for you.
A waiter writing down multiple orders before taking them to the kitchen, rather than running back and forth for each individual order.
A directory is like the index section of a phone book - it doesn't contain the actual information but tells you where to find each person's details.
